JavaScript Core Concepts (78) - Promise - Promise and AJAX

Where do you actually run into Promise the most? AJAX. So this chapter walks through how Promise and AJAX fit together — wrapping a raw XMLHttpRequest call in a Promise so you can chain requests one after another and know for sure each one finished before the next kicks off.
JavaScript Core Concepts (77) - Promise - Handy Promise Tricks

Now let's look at a couple of Promise APIs you'll actually reach for in real projects. This post covers Promise.all and Promise.race — how they work, what they return, and the one key difference between them that trips people up.
JavaScript Core (76) - Promise Chaining: How then and catch Actually Flow

Let's keep going with Promises. This time it's all about Promise chaining — how you stack one then after another, how returning a value (or a Promise) passes data down the chain, what happens when a reject jumps straight to catch, and how you can keep the chain running even after catching an error. We'll also look at the two-callback form of then.
JavaScript Core (74) - Promise - The Basics of Promise

In the last post we figured out why we even need Promise and how the old way of writing async code differed. This time we start getting hands-on with the Promise syntax itself — the three states, what resolve and reject actually do, and how then and catch fit into the picture.
JavaScript Core Concepts (75) - Promise - Building Your Own Promise

We've already covered the basics of Promise and written a few of them, but honestly the best way to make it stick is to build one from scratch yourself. This post walks through how to create your own Promise, how resolve and reject work, and why JavaScript doesn't wait around for a Promise to finish before moving on.
Copy Text to the Clipboard with JavaScript (a Few Ways to Do It)

In real-world dev you often need a button that copies a chunk of text — think coupon codes or order numbers. This post walks through the different ways to copy text to the clipboard in JavaScript, from the old document.execCommand approach (with input fields and Range objects) to the newer, cleaner Clipboard API.
How to Switch ESLint Style (Standard → Airbnb) Without Losing Your Mind

When you build a project you'll almost always reach for a style manager to keep everyone's code consistent — on the frontend that's ESLint. The thing is, once you've picked a style at the start, switching to a different one later is a pain. So here's how to move ESLint from the Standard style over to Airbnb.
The JavaScript a = b = c Interview Question, Finally Explained

Multiple left-hand assignment — also called chain variable assignments — is one of those classic JavaScript interview questions. Stuff like a = b = c that looks innocent but trips people up. So I wrote a post digging into how it actually works, including the nasty version that mixes in hoisting and call-by-sharing object references.
A Friendly Intro to Functional Programming in JavaScript

Functional Programming is honestly one of the more fun paradigms to play with. But why do we even need it? In short, it's about avoiding a bunch of problems caused by side effects. So I figured I'd write a really beginner- friendly, really simple intro to Functional Programming in JavaScript.
JavaScript Core Concepts (73) - Promise - Why Do We Even Need Promises?

Time to get into Promises. Honestly, you've probably been using them in real projects already — you just didn't realize that thing you were calling was wrapped in a Promise. This post walks through why Promises exist and how they compare to the old jQuery-style async code.
